data.title: CMS launches Outcome-Based Care Blueprint to improve Medicare, reduce costs. TEAM model begins Jan 1, 2026 at 742 hospitals, targeting $19.2 billion in expenses.
data.description: Today, a final rule was issued aimed at enhancing health outcomes and cutting costs for millions of Americans receiving the five most expensive medical procedures. The new Transforming Episode Accountability Model (TEAM) is set to be implemented on January 1, 2026, at 742 hospitals across the nation. Its goal is to reduce the $19.2 billion currently spent on these procedures.

data.title: Louisiana Fish Fry names Joe Tuza as new CEO after 3 decades of experience.
data.description: The appointment of Joe Tuza as Chief Executive Officer has been announced by Louisiana Fish Fry ("LFF"), a top manufacturer and distributor of authentic Louisiana-inspired food products. With over three decades of experience, Tuza will lead the company in Baton Rouge, La., starting July 31, 2025.

data.title: Researcher proposes a ‘Lean’ network for Ethereum to boost speed, resist quantum threats, and simplify the layer-1 blockchain.
data.description: The proposal seeks to enhance Ethereum's speed and make it quantum-resistant, as well as to simplify the technical complexities of the layer-1 blockchain.
Amtrak: Philadelphia-Wilmington Rail Traffic Halted

data.title: M-tron Industries to release Q2 2025 financials on August 12, 2025; Hosting conference call on August 13, 2025.
data.description: In Orlando, Florida, on July 31, 2025, M-tron Industries, Inc. (NYSE American: MPTI), a leading U.S. provider of radio frequency products for aerospace & defense, avionics, and space sectors, is set to announce its financial performance for the second quarter.

data.title: Enovix reported Q2 revenue of $7.47M, exceeding estimates; total revenue up 98% YoY due to strong demand for products.
data.description: Enovix Corp (NASDAQ: ENVX) released their second quarter financial results after market close on Thursday, causing a downturn in after-hours trading. In Q2, the company reported a revenue of $7.47 million, surpassing estimates of $5.57 million. The adjusted earnings per share for the quarter stood at a loss of 13 cents, beating the projected loss of 17 cents. Total revenue experienced a significant 98% increase compared to the previous year, attributed to strong demand for Enovix's lithium-ion batteries. The company showcased the successful launch of its AI-1 product. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.
South Korea foreign minister discussing summit with US: Yonhap

data.title: Dow Jones futures steady as tech-led rally fades. Amazon, Apple release earnings. Jobs report awaited.
data.description: Futures for the Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq remained relatively stable as investors awaited the release of the July jobs report. Amazon.com and Apple took the spotlight with their earnings reports on Thursday night. Following a tech-driven surge, these companies, along with Reddit, played significant roles in market movement. The jobs report data was eagerly anticipated amid the changing market conditions.

data.title: Stock market indexes fluctuated, Amazon rose before earnings, Apple declined. IPO Figma surged 277%. Copper futures dropped, Nvidia's partner Arm fell 11%.
data.description: Copper futures experienced a consecutive two-day drop. Arm, a partner of Nvidia, saw a significant decline of nearly 11% in trading volume. The stock market witnessed indexes shifting downwards, with Amazon's stock rising in anticipation of earnings, while Apple's performance faded. Meanwhile, the IPO of Figma saw a remarkable 277% increase.

data.title: AES reports strong Q2 2025 results; Renewables SBU's EBITDA up 56% YoY. On track to add 3.2 GW with 1.9 GW completed and 1.3 GW nearly finished.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, the Adjusted EBITDA of the Renewables SBU saw a significant growth of 56% compared to the same period in 2024. The company is progressing well towards its goal of adding 3.2 gigawatts (GW) of new projects in operation by the end of 2025. So far, 1.9 GW have been completed, with the remaining 1.3 GW being 78% complete. Since the last quarterly update in May, new projects with a substantial capacity have been signed or awarded.

data.title: Canfor Pulp reports Q2 2025 loss of $5M, net loss of $7M ($0.10 per share) due to trade uncertainties, global economic pressures.
data.description: Canfor Pulp Products Inc. (CPPI) reported an operating loss of $5 million and a net loss of $7 million in the second quarter of 2025, equivalent to $0.10 per share. Global pulp market fundamentals weakened due to trade uncertainties, particularly in China, and rising global economic uncertainty affected North American kraft paper markets. The Canadian dollar strengthened by 4% against the US dollar, impacting sales unit realizations. Sales for Q2 2025 were $177.9 million, a decrease from $196.2 million in Q1 2025. Operating income before amortization was $3.5 million, a decrease from $21.3 million in Q1 2025. Adjusted operating income before amortization was $6.4 million. These results reflect challenging market conditions for CPPI in the second quarter.

data.title: Kodiak Gas Services to report Q2 2025 financial results on August 6th after market closes, with conference call on Aug 7th.
data.description: Kodiak Gas Services, Inc., a prominent provider of energy infrastructure and contract compression services, disclosed its plan to unveil its financial results for the second quarter of 2025 on August 6, 2025, post-market hours. Following this release, the company will conduct a conference call and webcast on August 7, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. Eastern Time (9:00 a.m. Central Time). Kodiak, listed as KGS on NYSE, operates as a key player in the sector.

data.title: Lincoln Educational Services Corp (Nasdaq: LINC) to discuss Q2 earnings on Aug 11, 2025, at 10:00 am Eastern time. News release before 9:30 am Eastern time.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, in Parsippany, New Jersey, Lincoln Educational Services Corporation (Nasdaq: LINC) revealed plans for a conference call to review its financial performance for the second quarter of 2025. The conference call is scheduled for August 11, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. Eastern time. Before the call at 9:30 a.m. Eastern time, a news release with a detailed overview of Lincoln's results will be published.

data.title: Rocket Companies (NYSE: RKT) beats Q2 revenue estimates at $1.34B, with EPS at 4 cents. $28.4B net rate lock volume. Closed loans at $29.1B.
data.description: Rocket Companies (NYSE: RKT) released its Q2 financial results, surpassing expectations with adjusted revenue of $1.34 billion, compared to the anticipated $1.28 billion. Adjusted earnings per share were four cents, above the estimated three cents per share. Rocket Mortgage saw a 13% YoY increase in net rate lock volume, reaching $28.4 billion. Closed loan origination volume for the quarter was $29.1 billion, up by 18% YoY. For more details, refer to the complete article.

data.title: Canfor Corp. reported Q2 2025 operating loss of $251M, shareholder net loss of $203M, with adjusted loss of $50.7M. Operations impacted by weak pricing, with permanent closure of sawmills. Global economic uncertainty affected market fundamentals, led to asset write-down. Vida AB announced $164M acquisition. Sales totaled $1,379.4M in Q2 2025, operating loss reported at $251.4M. CEO highlighted challenges in N. American market. For full story, visit Benzinga.com.
data.description: Canfor Corporation reported a Q2 2025 operating loss of $251 million, with a net loss of $203 million or $1.71 per share. Adjusting for items, the operating loss was $51 million, compared to $32 million in Q1 2025. The closure of sawmills led to an asset write-down of $189 million, with restructuring costs of $7 million. Sales for Q2 2025 were $1.379 billion, with an operating loss of $251.4 million. Canfor President stated that while European operations were strong, North America faced challenges. Vida AB agreed to purchase AB Karl Hedin Sågverk for $164 million. These financial struggles were attributed to sluggish demand and weak pricing environments.

data.title: Lawsuit filed against Sierra Mountain Logistics for not providing meal and rest breaks to employees, resulting in missed payments.
data.description: Sierra Mountain Logistics, Inc. is facing allegations of not offering employees the mandatory meal and rest breaks during their shifts. This failure to provide breaks reportedly led to employees not receiving their entitled meal premium payments.

data.title: Nektar Therapeutics (Nasdaq: NKTR) to reveal Q2 results on August 7, 2025, after U.S. market hours. CEO Howard Robin to host.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, Nektar Therapeutics (Nasdaq: NKTR) is set to reveal its second quarter financial results on Thursday, August 7, 2025, following the closure of U.S. financial markets. Howard Robin, the President and CEO, will lead a discussion.

data.title: Lifeway Foods named Processor of the Year by Dairy Foods for revolutionizing US kefir category. #growth #LWAY
data.description: Lifeway Foods, Inc., a prominent supplier of kefir and fermented dairy products based in Morton Grove, Illinois, has recently received the prestigious title of Processor of the Year from Dairy Foods Magazine. This recognition, which comes in the midst of a thriving business year for Lifeway, demonstrates the company's exceptional performance in the industry. As a publicly traded company under the symbol NASDAQ: LWAY, Lifeway continues to uphold its position as a leader in the market, providing high-quality products to consumers.
